$HOOD at IPO (July 2021):
• $38/share
• 11M funded accounts
• $44B assets under custody
• $713M annual revenue
• Unprofitable

$HOOD today:
• $66/share
• 27M funded accounts
• $322B assets under custody
• $4.4B annual revenue
• ~2.1B gross profit run-rate

Up just 74% from the IPO price despite revenue growing more than 6x, assets under custody growing more than 7x & the business now being profitable.

This macro washout over the past six months has created a lot of potential opportunity across popular growth names:

$RKLB just completed its 85th Electron launch for the European Space Agency.

The mission deployed the first two satellites for “Celeste” which is Europe’s next-gen LEO navigation system.

Great to be featured in The Washington Post discussing why $TSLA could be a major winner in the new AI economy.

My view is that Tesla is building the infrastructure layer for physical AI with a neural network that could eventually power both mobility and robotics.
$PANW CEO just bought $10M worth of stock at ~$147 per share.
Me heading into the weekend with $QQQ in a correction and Monday’s implied move at 2% trying to act like I’ll be able to relax.

$NVDA H100 rental prices have climbed to $2.59 per hour which is the highest spot rate in 18 months.

Not bad for a chip architecture that was announced four years ago.

$GOOGL is nearing a deal to help finance a multibillion-dollar Texas data center leased to Anthropic. 

The site is expected to reach 500 MW by late 2026 and could eventually scale to 7.7 GW using on-site gas power.

I’m in the camp that thinks the doomsday scenario being priced across software after every new model release has gone too far, especially with the whole sector being painted with the same brush.

That said, it is interesting that we still have not seen any major private equity firm step in to acquire these distressed software names trading below 3x sales with healthy margins... especially when they were comfortable doing deals above 10x sales last year. 

To me that reads the fear is still high enough that even the most opportunistic capital in the world is standing at the edge of the pool deciding whether to jump.

$MSFT is taking 900 megawatts of data center capacity at Crusoe’s Abilene, Texas project which is a site previously tied to $ORCL & OpenAI.

The first building is expected to be ready and powered by mid-2027.

"If something goes wrong on the CapEx front, on the investment front, on the ROI front, you pick it. The entire market, bonds, credits, structured products, etc...

I don't want to say we're in for a world of hurt, but there is going to be a bear market when this ends."

🎙️ @DanGreenhaus

"Let's just say on Friday we sign a cease fire and all hostilities cease. We are not going back to 60 bucks (per barrel). There's going to be a geopolitical risk premium built into the price of oil... You will have, for the foreseeable future, a more permanent, higher plateau, not just because of the geopolitical risk premium, but you've also done enormous damage to 6-9 refineries."

🛢️ @DanGreenhaus

Nearby Brent Crude Oil Futures made a new closing high today. Up 59% since the war began.

As a follow up, I'm not going to mince words ...

If the real reason for the delay is that the military assets needed were not in place, then don't make up shit about 10 tankers going through the Strait (no evidence) or the Iranians asked for the delay (the Pakistanis, who are the intermediaries, said no such request was made).

Say you'll keep talking, you're losing patience, and say every day the Iranians don't deal means the Marines are getting closer to the Middle East.

The market would understand that, and the crude oil response (fall and stay down) might have been more favorable.

This just looks like a TACO mess.

The fastest way to go broke in this business is to believe the regulars on @CNBC actually care about your P&L. Alpha is built through a disciplined process, not in a makeup chair.
Forgetting that CNBC even exists at all has been a huge blessing in my life

Wow sitting here reflecting on where i was a decade ago starting to learn the process. And today long bond market volatility, weat, corn adding more thru COM,  bunch of oil and anchoring on buxx and spaxx…. up .30 today on a train wreck type day to the tourist. Thanks Hedgeye….
Hedgeye's community can evidently make money when everyone else is losing theirs
CNBC-type amateurs grade the process one position at a time. Pros grade the portfolio over a Full Investing Cycle. That's not a philosophy difference. That's the whole ballgame
Thank you @KeithMcCullough and @Hedgeye - the process works.  Another up day in a down market.  Have a blessed weekend.

Broadcom executive Natarajan Ramachandran said that the chipmaker is “seeing that TSMC is hitting (production capacity) limits,” adding that while TSMC is adding capacity in 2027, it “has become a bottleneck, or that has kind of choked ⁠the supply chain in 2026.”

$SPX futures -27 as yesterday’s weakness gets follow-through. $SPX broke 6525 and is now below last Friday’s low of 6473, with tech pressuring the tape and many names at new March lows. Time to get tactical when $SPX breaks the 8/21-day (~6811)—we just spent 5 days under the 200-day.

A YEAR OR TWO?

WE FUCKED.
They will do everything to distract you from Epstein.
AND WHERE THE FUCK IS THE XRP ARMY

No. 2 weeks before the October low in 2022 I fucking screamed melt-up.

And I was fucking right then.

Gold at $4,439/oz.

Not a trade. Not momentum. Crisis pricing.

When gold runs this far while $SPY and $TLT both sell off, the market isn't looking for yield. It's looking for survival.

In 2006, certain people argued a housing crisis would crash the stock market.

They were wrong for 2 years, until Lehman wiped out a FUCKING DECADE OF STOCK MARKET RETURNS.

2 years wrong, 10 years right.

THAT is an example that proves this line of thinking is fucking wrong.
My algorithm silenced you. You’re back now. I’m all on board. Always have been. Let’s go to the FUCKING TRENCHES.
You’ve got a better holistic grasp on how financial markets are interconnected than anyone we’ve seen on X… and you’ve never relented…  despite all the hate. Inspiring.

$DXY above 100. Dollar firming while everything else breaks.

A strong dollar is a global tax — on EM debt, on commodities priced in USD, on multinationals.

$EEM already bleeding. Who's next?

USDJPY approaching 160.

The last time carry trades unwound from this level, markets didn't just dip — they broke.

$VIX is at 31. $SPY is cracking. The yen is the fuse. Watch it.

Wait so Japan will panic.

I’m right mother fuckers.

I was early, yes.

But I’m fucking right.

You don’t get out of this without a massive deleverage event.

The Main Event.
I NEVER DOUBTED YOU & LOOK AT ALL THESE PEOPLE COMING OUT OF THE WOODWORKS USING YOUR WORDS

$TLT at 85.50. 10Y yield hit 4.48 intraday — highest since July 2025.

Stocks falling. Bonds falling.

The "60/40 is dead" crowd has been right for the wrong reasons. Now they're right for the right ones.
